todaysEvents.summary: Iran submits new Hormuz proposal via Pakistan to US (Apr 27): reopen strait if US lifts blockade and war ends; nuclear talks deferred to later phase. Araghchi meets Putin in St. Petersburg (Apr 27); Putin pledges full support for Iran. Most concrete Iranian Hormuz offer yet (Axios, NPR, Al Jazeera, Foreign Policy, Fortune, Apr 27).
todaysEvents.delta: Fractional de-escalation: Iran's concrete Hormuz offer is positive signal; tail risk revised to 45% from 47%; but US rejection limits impact

todaysEvents.summary: Rubio calls Iran deal 'unacceptable' - US rejects phased approach; Rubio: 'any agreement must definitively prevent Iran from sprinting towards a nuclear weapon'; Trump unlikely to accept Iran's proposal per sources (CNBC, CNN, Apr 27). Germany's Merz: Iran 'humiliating' US as talks stall (Irish Times). Diplomatic window narrows on nuclear precondition.
todaysEvents.delta: Negative: US firm rejection of nuclear exclusion removes short-term breakthrough prospect; Brent sells off ~4% on rejection news

todaysEvents.summary: Ceasefire nominally active; US blockade and IRGC Hormuz enforcement both intact; Iran holds 3 Western vessels (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas, Euphoria); mine-clearing ~6 months (PBS); Brent confirmed $108.11 Apr 27 (+2.64%, TradingEconomics); est. ~$102 Apr 28 (-3.8% on Rubio rejection); tail risk 45%.
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral/Lateral: Physical Hormuz access unchanged; Iran offer positive but US rejection offsets; structural disruption intact

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran Hormuz proposal (Apr 27) creates framework for phased deal - Rubio's rejection not final; Oman back-channel and Russia support keep window open. Iran accepts modified framework with nuclear addendum; vessels returned; Hormuz reopens. Narrow (10%) - Rubio firm on nuclear precondition; Araghchi calling US demands 'maximalist'; mine-clearing ~6 months regardless of deal (PBS); full commercial reopening delayed. Brent retreats toward $92-98/bbl on deal; iTraxx tightens toward 175bp.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Iran-US nuclear stalemate persists - Rubio's rejection of phased Hormuz deal blocks progress; Araghchi shuttle (Moscow, Oman) continues without breakthrough; US blockade and IRGC Hormuz enforcement co-exist; selective bilateral transit; periodic vessel seizures. Brent stabilises $95-110/bbl; JKM $17-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 195-215bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (45%).

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Rubio rejection triggers return to escalation - US insistence on nuclear inclusion rejected by Iran; ceasefire formally terminated; IRGC ROE hardens; 'shoot and kill' order triggers incident with IRGC small boat. Gulf-wide blockade activated; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis. Tail 45% (down from 47%) - Iran's concrete Hormuz offer reduces probability vs prior stalemate, but US rejection partially reverses.
